perturbation.original_text: Each Party acknowledges that monetary damages would be inadequate to protect the other Party against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, and, without prejudice to any other rights and remedies otherwise available to the other Party, agrees to the granting of injunctive relief in favour of th e other Party without proof of actual damages.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall pay its own costs and expenses incurred in connection with this Agreement. Each Party acknowledges that monetary damages would be inadequate to protect the other Party against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, and, without prejudice to any other rights and remedies otherwise available to the other Party, agrees to the granting of injunctive relief in favour of th e other Party without proof of actual damages.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the injunctive relief acknowledgement into the section related to costs and expenses obfuscates the importance of the clause, suggesting that remedies are limited to costs, thus decreasing its enforceability.
perturbation.law_explanation: Rule 10.35 details the specifics regarding injunctive relief applications. By placing the clause regarding such relief within costs and expenses, it doesn't give the clause the focus it needs, especially if a party reviewing the contract only skims, making it less apparent and harder to locate if a breach happens, hindering its effectiveness.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation is categorized as a 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction'. The original text clearly states the availability of injunctive relief. The changed text moves this acknowledgment into a section concerning costs and expenses, which obfuscates the importance and enforceability of the injunctive relief clause. The Law Explanation states that this placement doesn't give the clause the focus it needs, potentially hindering its effectiveness if a breach occurs. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction' as the required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. Therefore, the modification creates a contradiction.
